text: The surgeon closes the fascial defect after performing lysis of adhesions and reducing the hernia contents.
The surgeon creates small stab incisions along the midline over the hernia defect and inserts a disposable suture passer device through these incisions.
The surgeon navigates the suture passer device through the subcutaneous tissue and orients it laterally to pierce through the edge of the fascia on one side of the hernia defect.
The surgeon uses a grasper to transfer the suture intra-abdominally, then reinserts the suture passer through the same punctured incision to pass the suture through the fascia on the opposite side.

The surgeon places sutures one centimeter apart along the midline of the fascial defect. After placing every four sutures, the abdomen is desufflated, and the sutures are tied extracorporeally to approximate the fascial edges.
The surgeon continues to place sutures along the midline, extending slightly beyond the cephalad and caudal ends of the hernia defect, ensuring complete closure of the fascial defect.
Twelve sero-PDF sutures are placed along the entire hernia defect, followed by securing the mesh in place using cardinal sero-PDF sutures at the four corners.
Permanent tags are placed using a double crown technique, securing the mesh in position over the hernia defect.
